title: Canonical Intent
description: The Canonical Intent is produced by Stage 3 (Structured Intent) of the Governed Execution Protocol. It represents the normalized, validated, and hashed form of an incoming request. It is consumed by Stage 4 (Policy & Risk Check), Stage 5 (Authorization), and Stage 6 (Execution Gate). The intent_id and canonical hash of this object are referenced in all downstream records including authorization tokens, receipts, and ledger entries.

intent_id.description: Unique identifier for this canonical intent (UUID v4)

request_id.description: Reference to the original request registered at Stage 1 (Intake)

actor_id.description: Verified identity of the requesting entity (DID URI or system identifier)

action_type.description: Canonical action type from the intent ontology (e.g., WIRE_TRANSFER, DATA_DELETE, CODE_DEPLOY)

target_resource.description: Identifier of the resource upon which the action is to be performed

parameters.description: Action-specific parameters normalized to standard formats (amounts, currencies, identifiers)

risk_category.description: Preliminary risk level assigned by Stage 2 (Classification)

timestamp.description: UTC Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the canonical intent was formed

nonce.description: Cryptographic nonce (minimum 128 bits) generated at Stage 1 (Intake) to prevent replay attacks
